assertions.description: The 'loading' property of a <video> element is a string.
The default reflected value of the 'loading' property on <video> is 'eager'.
Setting 'loading' to 'lazy' on <video> correctly reflects to property and attribute.
assertions.test: (() => { const v = document.createElement('video'); v.loading = 'lazy'; return v.loading === 'lazy' && v.getAttribute('loading') === 'lazy'; })()

assertions.description: Setting 'loading' to 'lazy' on <audio> correctly reflects to property and attribute.
assertions.test: (() => { const a = document.createElement('audio'); a.loading = 'lazy'; return a.loading === 'lazy' && a.getAttribute('loading') === 'lazy'; })()

assertions.description: Setting an invalid 'loading' value on <video> falls back to reflecting 'eager'.
assertions.test: (() => { const v = document.createElement('video'); v.loading = 'invalid-value'; return v.loading === 'eager' && v.getAttribute('loading') === 'invalid-value'; })()

assertions.description: Setting an invalid 'loading' value on <audio> falls back to reflecting 'eager'.
assertions.test: (() => { const a = document.createElement('audio'); a.loading = 'invalid-value'; return a.loading === 'eager' && a.getAttribute('loading') === 'invalid-value'; })()
